title: Implement PII protection for CloudWatch logs

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## Scope

Implement CloudWatch Logs data protection policies to mask sensitive data in console chat logs (and eventually all Plexus CloudWatch logging).

## Problem

Console chat llm-context logs will contain full conversation history, which may include:
- User-provided PII (names, addresses, phone numbers, emails)
- Credentials (API keys, passwords accidentally pasted)
- PHI (protected health information in healthcare contexts)
- Financial data (credit cards, account numbers)
- Device identifiers, national IDs, passport numbers

Without masking, this violates data protection policies and compliance requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS).

## AWS CloudWatch Data Protection

AWS supports managed data protection with automatic masking:

**Sensitive data types covered**:
- **Credentials**: API keys, passwords, auth tokens
- **Device identifiers**: IMEI, MAC addresses
- **Financial**: Credit cards, bank accounts, SWIFT codes
- **PHI**: Medical record numbers, health plan IDs, prescription numbers
- **PII**: 
  - Driver's license numbers
  - National ID numbers (SSN, passport, taxpayer IDs)
  - Email addresses, phone numbers
  - Physical addresses, ZIP codes

**Reference**: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/protect-sensitive-log-data.html

## Implementation Plan

**Step 1: Create data protection policy**
- Use AWS managed data identifiers for common PII/PHI types
- Define custom patterns for Plexus-specific sensitive data (if any)
- Configure masking strategy (redact vs hash vs audit-only)

**Step 2: Apply to log groups**
- `/plexus/procedures/{account_id}` - procedure logs
- Any future console chat log groups

**Step 3: Infrastructure as Code**
- Add CloudWatch data protection policy to CDK/Terraform
- Apply to all Plexus log groups by pattern

**Step 4: Test masking**
- Log test messages with known PII patterns
- Verify masking in CloudWatch console
- Ensure legitimate debugging data still visible

## Policy Configuration (Example)

```json
{
  "Name": "PlexusLogDataProtection",
  "Description": "Mask PII/PHI/credentials in Plexus logs",
  "Version": "2021-06-01",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "AuditAndRedact",
      "DataIdentifier": [
        "arn:aws:dataprotection::aws:data-identifier/EmailAddress",
        "arn:aws:dataprotection::aws:data-identifier/CreditCardNumber",
        "arn:aws:dataprotection::aws:data-identifier/SsnUs",
        "arn:aws:dataprotection::aws:data-identifier/ApiKey",
        "arn:aws:dataprotection::aws:data-identifier/AwsSecretKey"
      ],
      "Operation": {
        "Audit": {
          "FindingsDestination": {}
        },
        "Deidentify": {
          "MaskConfig": {}
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Dependencies

- Requires plx-ecc9bf (logger refactor) to be complete
- Should be applied before console chat logging goes live

## Testing

- [ ] Test with known PII patterns (fake SSN, email, phone, credit card)
- [ ] Verify masking appears in CloudWatch
- [ ] Verify audit trail of masked data (findings destination)
- [ ] Confirm no false positives on legitimate debugging data
- [ ] Load test: verify no performance impact

## Compliance Notes

This is a **compliance blocker** - console chat logging should NOT go to production without PII protection in place.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implementation complete: Applied data protection policy programmatically in PlexusCloudWatchLogger using put_data_protection_policy() with AWS managed data identifiers. Created test script for verification.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Verified comprehensive PII masking working: emails, names, phones, SSN, credit cards, IP addresses, and street addresses all masked successfully. Policy includes 100+ AWS managed data identifiers for global PII/PHI protection.
